description: This figure presents electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) characterization of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (LDSs) subjected to solution treatments at varying temperatures. The panel comprises four rows representing samples S0 (as-rolled), S950 (950°C), S1050 (1050°C), and S1150 (1150°C), each containing three maps: inverse pole figure (IPF) maps (a-d) revealing grain orientation and morphology, kernel average misorientation (KAM) maps (a₁-d₁) indicating local residual stress distribution, and grain boundary (GB) distribution maps (a₂-d₂) distinguishing low-angle (2-10°, blue) and high-angle (>10°, red) boundaries. The IPF maps demonstrate that S0 exhibits heterogeneous grain sizes with deformed structures, while solution treatment produces equiaxed austenite grains that coarsen from approximately 8.7 μm (S950) to 32.0 μm (S1150). KAM analysis shows S0 has elevated residual stress (average 1.62) which reduces to ~0.4 after recrystallization. GB maps reveal S0 contains abundant LAGBs, whereas solution-treated samples display high densities of annealing twin boundaries (Σ3) and HAGBs in S950, progressively decreasing with grain growth. These microstructural modifications—particularly the optimized grain boundary density in S950—facilitate preferential aluminum dissolution at boundaries, enabling rapid formation of dense Al-rich oxide films that enhance corrosion resistance.
